FORM 8.5 (EPT/NON-RI)
PUBLIC OPENING POSITION DISCLOSURE/DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN
EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITHOUT RECOGNISED INTERMEDIARY ("RI") STATUS (OR WHERE RI STATUS IS NOT APPLICABLE)
Rule 8.5 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")
1. KEY INFORMATION
(a) Name of exempt principal trader:
Cavendish Securities Plc
(b) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:
Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree
AUDIOBOOM GROUP PLC
(c) Name of the party to the offer with which exempt principal trader is connected:
AUDIOBOOM GROUP PLC
(d) Date position held/dealing undertaken:
For an opening position disclosure, state the latest practicable date prior to the disclosure
17/03/2026
(e) In addition to the company in 1(b) above, is the exempt principal trader making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?
If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"
N/A
2. POSITIONS OF THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER
If there are positions or rights to subscribe to disclose in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a) or (b) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security.
(a) Interests and short positions in the relevant securities of the offeror or offeree to which the disclosure relates following the dealing (if any)
Class of relevant security:
ORD 0.5P

All interests and all short positions should be disclosed.
Details of any open stock-settled derivative positions (including traded options), or agreements to purchase or sell relevant securities, should be given on a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions).
(b) Rights to subscribe for new securities (including directors' and other employee options)
Class of relevant security in relation to which subscription right exists:
Details, including nature of the rights concerned and relevant percentages:
3. DEALINGS (IF ANY) BY THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER
Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 3(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.
The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.

Class of relevant security

e.g. call option

Details

4. OTHER INFORMATION
(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements
Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:
Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives
Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:
(i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or
(ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:
If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(c) Attachments
Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?
NO

18/03/2026
Contact name:
Umika Patel

+44 (0) 20 7220 0529

latest.title: Block admission six monthly return
latest.content:
11 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0985W
Audioboom Group PLC
11 March 2026
Audioboom Group plc
("Audioboom" or the "Company")
Block admission six monthly return
Audioboom (AIM: BOOM), the leading global podcast company, announces the following information in connection with its block admission pursuant to Rule 29 and Schedule Six of the AIM Rules for Companies.
Name of company:
Audioboom Group plc
Name of scheme:
Audioboom Group plc Share Option Scheme 2014, as continued by the Audioboom Group plc 2025 Employee Share Plan
Period of return:
From 9 September 2025 to 8 March 2026
Number and class of securities not issued under the scheme at the beginning of the period:
1,656,291
ordinary shares of no par value in the Company (the "Ordinary Shares")
Number and class of securities admitted to the scheme during the period:
No options over Ordinary Shares were granted during the period
Number of securities issued under the scheme during the period:
12,500 Ordinary Shares
Number of options under the scheme that have lapsed during the period:
Nil options have lapsed over Ordinary Shares
Balance under the scheme of securities not yet issued at the end of the period:
1,643,791 Ordinary Shares
Number and class of securities originally admitted and the date of admission:
1,337,415 Ordinary Shares were admitted on 6 March 2020
Subsequent admissions to increase the block listing:
358,000 Ordinary Shares (
184,000 on 21 October 2020, 174,000 over March 2021 to August 2021)
Number of shares admitted to trading under the blocklisting:
689,506 Ordinary Shares
Remaining shares available under the blocklisting:
1,005,909 Ordinary Shares
Contact name and telephone number:
Brad Clarke, Chief Financial Officer
+44(0)20 3714 4285
Enquiries
Audioboom Group plc
Stuart Last, Chief Executive Officer
Brad Clarke, Chief Financial Officer
Tel: +44(0)20 3714 4285
Cavendish Capital Markets Ltd (Nominated Adviser and Broker)
Jonny Franklin-Adams/Fergus Sullivan/Elysia Bough (Corporate Finance)
Harriet Ward (ECM)
Tel: +44(0)20 7220 0500
J Goodwin & Co (Financial Advisers to Audioboom)
Rupert Hill / Charlie Barnes Yallowley Harriet Ward (ECM)
Tel: +44(0)20 3976 6215
Rockefeller Capital Management (Financial Advisers to Audioboom)
William B. Drewry / Francisco A. Mato
Tel: +1 212-549-5341
Montfort (Financial PR Adviser to Audioboom)
James Olley
Tel: +44(0)7974 982302
Jack Hickman
Tel: +44(0)7736 201582
About Audioboom
Audioboom is a global leader in podcasting - our shows are downloaded and viewed 150 million times each month by 50 million unique listeners around the world. Audioboom is ranked as the fifth largest podcast publisher in the US by Edison Research.
Audioboom's ad-tech and monetisation platform underpins a scalable content business that provides commercial, distribution, marketing and production services for a premium network of top tier podcasts. Key partners include the official Formula 1 podcasts 'F1: Beyond the Grid' and 'F1 Nation', 'True Crime Obsessed' (US), 'The Tim Dillon Show' (US), 'No Such Thing As A Fish' (UK) and 'The Cycling Podcast' (UK).
Audioboom operates internationally, with global partnerships across North America, Europe, Asia and Australia. The platform distributes content via Apple Podcasts, YouTube, Spotify, Pandora, Amazon Music, Google Podcasts, iHeartRadio, Facebook and Twitter as well as a partner's own websites and mobile apps.
For more information, visit audioboom.com.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: Audioboom runs a cloud‑based platform that lets podcasters host, distribute and monetize shows.  Creators upload content, the service delivers it to listeners via apps, web players and RSS feeds, and the company offers advertising and subscription tools to turn audiences into revenue.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global podcast market is growing faster than most other media segments, with ad spend and premium subscriptions rising each year.  Audioboom’s technology gives podcasters a scalable way to reach audiences, and its monetization tools help the industry move from free listening to paid models—an attractive proposition for advertisers looking for high‑engagement content.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still generating revenue and has recently secured new commercial partnerships, but it has experienced a steep decline in share price that has pushed the stock near its lowest point of the year.  The business is expanding its content network while continuing to refine its ad‑tech stack, yet the market has reacted sharply to the recent downturn.

rns_narrative: Audioboom has announced a new exclusive partnership with Crooked Media, a high‑profile podcast network, which expands its content library and opens up new monetization channels.

The company has filed several regulatory updates (Form 8.3 and 8.5) that confirm its compliance posture and provide a clearer view of its financials, while the 2025 Trading Update gives investors a snapshot of revenue trends and cash flow.
A block admission notice suggests a fresh tranche of shares is being added to the market, potentially easing liquidity concerns and signalling confidence from insiders.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the community is largely silent but the few voices that speak are leaning toward a rebound after the partnership news.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls argue that the Crooked Media deal will boost traffic and advertising revenue, and that the podcast market’s growth trajectory should help Audioboom recover its valuation.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to intense competition from Spotify, Apple and other platforms, a thin profit margin on advertising, and the risk that the partnership may not translate into significant top‑line growth.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially neutral, the sentiment shifted to slightly bullish after the partnership announcement, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – there is no public buzz, which is a classic contrarian green light.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming Q2 earnings release – if guidance shows revenue growth or a stronger ad‑tech pipeline, it could spark a price move.
Any new monetization or distribution deal announced by Crooked Media or other networks that expands Audioboom’s reach.
A breakout above the recent low that is accompanied by a surge in trading volume, signalling institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that exceeds the 20‑day average, a close above the last swing low, and a positive earnings surprise.  Any announcement of a new Spotify integration or a significant licensing deal would also confirm the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle – the price is near its deepest point, and the APEX score suggests a tight compression that could release a spring.  A mean‑reversion bounce is likely once a catalyst confirms that the business model is solidifying.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share has fallen more than 70% from its peak, which historically gives the highest return when a bounce occurs.  If the stock recovers to a level that covers the 52‑week low, the upside is roughly 2‑3× the current price.  The recent partnership and consistent regulatory filings reinforce the belief that the business fundamentals are intact and ready to benefit from a growing podcast market.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The podcast space is crowded, and ad revenue growth is slowing for many platforms.  If Audioboom fails to convert its new content deals into higher earnings or if competitors launch superior monetization tools, the price could stay trapped below its low.  The worst‑case scenario is a further decline to a new support level, but the downside is capped by the current price level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 2–3× current price.  Potential downside: limited to the current price (no further loss beyond the present level).  The asymmetry is clear – a modest downside risk for a substantial upside if a catalyst triggers a rebound.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed volume spike and price break above the last swing low.  Use a position size that limits exposure to 5–10% of your equity, and set a stop just below the most recent low.  Take partial profits at 50% of the target and trail the stop to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a small hedge until the Q2 earnings or a new partnership announcement.  If earnings exceed expectations or a new deal is announced, consider a quick entry with a tight stop.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has been identified; the stock is not delisting or filing for bankruptcy.  Therefore, a contrarian bullish stance is justified.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Audioboom is a deep‑crashed podcast platform with a promising partnership and a quiet market – the right conditions for a sizable rebound if the next earnings or deal announcement confirms its growth path.
